questions.question: What distinguishes a unit rate from a general mathematical rate?
options: A unit rate compares identical units.
A unit rate has exactly 1 as its second (denominator) term.
A unit rate is always written in colon notation.
A unit rate must use decimal values.
questions.explanation: A unit rate is a rate where the second quantity is simplified to exactly 1 unit (e.g. per hour, per ounce).

questions.question: A jogger runs $15$ miles in $3$ hours. What is their unit running speed?
questions.explanation: Divide the distance by the time: $\frac{15 \text{ miles}}{3 \text{ hours}} = 5$ miles per hour.

questions.question: If a $5\text{-pound}$ bag of potatoes costs $4.50$ dollars, what is the unit price per pound?
questions.explanation: Divide cost by weight: $\frac{4.50 \text{ dollars}}{5 \text{ pounds}} = 0.90$ dollars per pound.

questions.question: A production line stamps $300$ parts in $15$ minutes. What is the unit stamping rate?
questions.explanation: Divide the total parts by the minutes: $\frac{300 \text{ parts}}{15 \text{ minutes}} = 20$ parts per minute.

questions.question: Which option is the better buy: a $10\text{-pack}$ of pens for $2.50$ dollars, or a $30\text{-pack}$ of the same pens for $6.00$ dollars?
options: The 10-pack of pens, because $2.50 is a smaller total cost.
The 30-pack of pens, because the unit cost is $0.20 per pen compared to $0.25.
The 10-pack, because the unit cost is $0.15 per pen.
questions.explanation: Store A (10-pack) unit cost is $\frac{2.50}{10} = 0.25$ per pen. Store B (30-pack) unit cost is $\frac{6.00}{30} = 0.20$ per pen. Lower unit cost is the better buy.
